evals.prompt: I have a 29-agent documentation pipeline under .github/agents/docwriter*.agent.md with an orchestrator, 6 coordinators, and specialists organized into 9 passes. The gap-hunter writes gap-analysis.json with re-entry targets, and the orchestrator cascade-resets passes before re-dispatching. Can you check whether the re-entry data actually flows through to the specialists, or if context is getting lost somewhere in the chain?
evals.expected_output: Should identify whether coordinators read gap-analysis.json during re-entry, whether specialists receive gap context in dispatch messages, and flag any dead branches in mode detection that guard re-entry handling. Should trace the full path: gap-hunter → verification-coordinator → orchestrator → target coordinator → specialists.

evals.prompt: My agent workflow has directives.md that gets read by both the orchestrator and coordinators. The architecture doc says coordinators read it directly, but I think some of my agent constraint sections might say the orchestrator relays it. Can you audit the directive propagation model for consistency across all agents and the architecture doc?
evals.expected_output: Should read all agents and the architecture doc, identify which model each component follows (relay vs direct-read), flag any contradictions between them, and verify specialists don't read directives.md directly. Should produce a findings list with exact file references.

evals.prompt: Run a full workflow evaluation on my fractal docwriter agent pipeline. Check everything — pass sequencing, data flow, re-entry logic, status schemas, directive propagation, timestamp handling, error handling. I want to know every inconsistency, dead code path, and missing data flow edge before I ship this.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a comprehensive findings report covering all 9 checklist categories. Should build the producer-consumer matrix, trace re-entry paths, verify status schemas, check directive propagation, and flag timestamp handling issues. Findings ordered by severity with file citations.
